steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is A, $5x^2 -2x + 3$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Distribute the minus sign
steps.content: Subtracting a polynomial means flipping every sign inside the second set of parentheses: $$(2x^2 - 4) - (-3x^2 + 2x - 7) = 2x^2 - 4 + 3x^2 - 2x + 7.$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Group by degree: $$x^2\text{-terms: } 2x^2 + 3x^2 = 5x^2.$$ $$x\text{-terms: } -2x.$$ $$\text{Constants: } -4 + 7 = 3.$$ So the result is $5x^2 - 2x + 3$.

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘$5x^2 -2x + 3$’: $5x^2 - 2x + 3$ — matches exactly. Kept.
  • Choice B — ‘$5x^2 + 2x -3$’: $5x^2 + 2x - 3$ — wrong sign on $x$-term and wrong constant. Eliminated.
  • Choice C — ‘$- x^2 -2x -11$’: $-x^2 - 2x - 11$ — wrong leading coefficient (would come from adding, not subtracting, with extra sign errors). Eliminated.
  • Choice D — ‘$- x^2 + 2x -11$’: $-x^2 + 2x - 11$ — same problem as C, and wrong on all three terms. Eliminated.
That confirms A is correct.
